candidate_summary: PIT-VIPER differential-signal evaluation diagnostic: per-query D_q = score(L_c, q) - score(L_b, q) using a fixed trivial baseline model (random/majority/fixed-prompt) on the same queries; aggregate D over benchmark as primary informativeness/signal-vs-noise diagnostic, identifying queries where the candidate scores only marginally above trivial baseline.

results_analysis.title: Signal and Noise: Reducing Uncertainty in Language Model Evaluation (2025/08)
results_analysis.reasoning: Defines signal as benchmark's discriminatory power separating better from worse models — the same informativeness/differential aggregate PIT-VIPER constructs by subtracting a trivial baseline per query. Triple match on kw, sem, and func.

results_analysis.title: Signal and Noise Framework (Emergent Mind)
results_analysis.reasoning: SNR-based design principles for benchmark utility — same conceptual artefact as PIT-VIPER's informativeness/signal diagnostic. Triple match.

results_analysis.title: When Judgment Becomes Noise: Design Failures in LLM Judge Benchmarks (2025/09)
results_analysis.reasoning: Concerned with benchmark noise / validity. Kw and sem both pass; func slightly below threshold because paper does not specifically propose paired-trivial differential.

results_analysis.title: Benchmark2: Systematic Evaluation of LLM Benchmarks (2026/01)
results_analysis.reasoning: Meta-eval of benchmark sets. Kw>=2 and sem at threshold 0.70 -> hit. Func below 0.7 since meta-eval differs from per-query differential.

results_analysis.title: Benchmarking LLMs' Judgments with No Gold Standard (2024/11)
results_analysis.reasoning: Two keyword matches ('benchmark', 'judgment/evaluation') satisfy kw>=2. Sem and func below thresholds, but kw rule triggers a hit.

results_analysis.title: The Anatomy of an LLM Benchmark
results_analysis.reasoning: Discusses IRT for selecting informative data — same informativeness diagnostic family PIT-VIPER inhabits. Kw>=2 -> hit even though sem/func just below 0.7.

results_analysis.title: A Practical Guide for Evaluating LLMs (2025/06)
results_analysis.reasoning: Discusses random-error vs systematic-bias decomposition — same concern as differentiating noise floor from informative signal. Kw>=2 -> hit.

results_analysis.title: When Can We Trust LLMs in Mental Health (2025/10)
results_analysis.reasoning: Domain-specific reliability study. No keyword cluster, sem and func well below thresholds. Not a hit.

verdict_sentence: Candidate FAILS: 7 of 8 results qualify as hits under hit=(kw>=2)OR(sem>=0.7)OR(func>=0.7); in particular the Signal and Noise framework (arxiv 2508.13144, sem 0.86, func 0.85) defines benchmark discriminatory power — the very informativeness/differential aggregate PIT-VIPER claims as novel, making the candidate's per-query paired-trivial baseline subtraction substantially anticipated by prior LLM-evaluation literature.
